DISCOVERY 302, NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS JOSE MARTINEZ, TATSIANA VETASHKINA, AND EDUARDO ROJAS' MOTION FOR ORDER REOPENING DISCOVERY IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MAY 21, 2013 TO GIVE PLAINTIFFS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFFS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 13, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. ANY PARTY WHO CONTESTS A TENTATIVE RULING MUST SEND AN EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO ALL OTHER PARTIES BY 4PM STATING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, THE PORTION(S) OF THE TENTATIVE RULING THAT THE PARTY CONTESTS. DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS JOSE MARTINEZ, TATSIANA VETASHKINA, AND EDUARDO ROJAS' MOTION FOR ORDER REOPENING DISCOVERY IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN GOOD CAUSE TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY. THE BASIS OF PLAINTIFFS REQUEST TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY IS THAT THEY WANT TO KNOW WHY DEFENDANTS SOUGHT TRIAL DE NOVO AFTER AN ARBITRATION AWARD. PLAINTIFFS CONTEND THAT FACTS MAY COME TO LIGHT WHICH WOULD FACILITATE FURTHER SETTLEMENT EFFORTS AND POSSIBLY IDENTIFY "SILVER BULLET" DEFENSES THAT WERE NOT RAISED IN ARBITRATION. THE REASONS PROFFERED DO NOT CONSTITUTE GOOD CAUSE IN THAT THEY WOULD APPLY IN ANY CASE IN WHICH A PARTY DID NOT ACCEPT AN ARBITRATION AWARD. FURTHER, PLAINTIFFS IN THEIR MOTION HAVE NOT IDENTIFIED THE SPECIFIC ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY THAT THEY WOULD UNDERTAKE IF DISCOVERY IS REOPENED ALTHOUGH THEIR PAPERS STATE THAT POST-ARBITRATION DISCOVERY REQUESTS WERE PROPOUNDED. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN GOOD CAUSE TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFFS OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF DEFENSE COUNSEL ARE DISMISSED AS THE COURT DID NOT RELY ON THE DECLARATION IN REACHING ITS DECISION. NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AS NONE WERE REQUESTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: MARK SCHREIBER; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion For Order Reopening Discovery
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiffs JOSE MARTINEZ, TATSIANA VETASHKINA, and EDUARDO ROJAS' Motion for Order Reopening Discovery is denied. Plaintiffs have not shown good cause to re-open discovery. The basis of plaintiffs request to re-open discovery is that they want to know why defendants sought trial de novo after an arbitration award. Plaintiffs contend that facts may come to light which would facilitate further settlement efforts and possibly identify "silver bullet" defenses that were not raised in arbitration. The reasons proffered do not constitute good cause in that they would apply in any case in which a party did not accept an arbitration award. Further, plaintiffs in their motion have not identified the specific additional discovery that they would undertake if discovery is reopened although their papers state that post-arbitration discovery requests were propounded. Accordingly, plaintiffs have not shown good cause to re-open discovery. Plaintiffs objections to the declaration of defense counsel are dismissed as the court did not rely on the declaration in reaching its decision. No sanctions are awarded as none were requested. Prevailing party to submit a form of order. Judge Pro Tem: Mark Schreiber; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion For Order Reopening Discovery
Off Calendar May-13-2013 Continued To May-21-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

Plaintiffs JOSE MARTINEZ, TATSIANA VETASHKINA, and EDUARDO ROJAS' Motion for Order Reopening Discovery is continued on the court's own motion to May 21, 2013 to give Plaintiffs the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiffs must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 13, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
